perturbation.original_text: Fees: The Fees for the Project shall be $279,000.00 Payment Schedule: Client shall pay Developer: 20% Deposit ($55,800.00) to be paid at the start of the project. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #1. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #2. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #3. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #4. *Milestone Delivery Schedule for Phase 1 Milestone #1 -January 1st, 2019 Milestone #2- February 1st, 2019. Milestone #3 - March 1st, 2019. Milestone #4 - April 1st, 2019.
perturbation.changed_text: Fees: The Fees for the Project shall be $279,000.00 Payment Schedule: Client shall pay Developer: 10% Deposit ($27,900.00) to be paid at the start of the project. 25% ($69,750.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #1. 15% ($41,850.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #2. 30% ($83,700.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #3. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #4. *Milestone Delivery Schedule for Phase 1 Milestone #1 -January 1st, 2019 Milestone #2- February 1st, 2019. Milestone #3 - March 1st, 2019. Milestone #4 - April 1st, 2019.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the percentage and amount due for each milestone payment. The original text specifies a consistent 20% ($55,800.00) payment for each milestone after the initial deposit, however, the new text uses different percentage and amounts values. This alteration causes confusion over the total payment and the amount due at each milestone. Furthermore, the change in percentages makes the math incorrect and total exceed the initial fee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fee Payment Schedule Fees: The Fees for the Project shall be $279,000.00 Payment Schedule: Client shall pay Developer: 10% Deposit ($27,900.00) to be paid at the start of the project. 25% ($69,750.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #1. 15% ($41,850.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #2. 30% ($83,700.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #3. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #4. *Milestone Delivery Schedule for Phase 1 Milestone #1 -January 1st, 2019 Milestone #2- February 1st, 2019. Milestone #3 - March 1st, 2019. Milestone #4 - April 1st, 2019.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the percentage and dollar amount due for each milestone payment, creating a contradiction with the original fee structure. The original text specified a consistent 20% payment for each milestone, whereas the revised text uses varying percentages and amounts. This discrepancy leads to confusion about the total payment and the amount due at each milestone, ultimately creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined in Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction. The contradicted text includes the changed values of the payments.

perturbation.original_text: We estimate that the website (Customer Portal) will take 3 months to complete because this will be the priority.
perturbation.changed_text: We estimate that the website (Customer Portal) will take 5 months to complete because this will be the priority.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the time estimate to complete the website. The modification from '3 months' to '5 months' creates uncertainty. The project schedule becomes ambiguous. This contradicts the original timeline and potentially impacts resource allocation and milestone targets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Based on the current requirements and this SOW, Seattle Software Developers estimates that this project will take around six [4] months of development, testing, and deployment. This estimate may change depending on unforeseen circumstances or requirements. We estimate that the website (Customer Portal) will take 5 months to complete because this will be the priority.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the estimated completion time for the website from 3 months to 5 months. The contradicted text includes the statement 'We estimate that the website (Customer Portal) will take 5 months to complete because this will be the priority' alongside 'Based on the current requirements and this SOW, Seattle Software Developers estimates that this project will take around six [4] months of development, testing, and deployment.' The change, therefore, creates an inconsistency with the estimated project time by introducing contradictory durations. The original text estimated 3 months but was changed to 5 months, and the contradicted text specifies 5 months for the website, but the project overall taking six. The estimated duration conflict is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Commission fees - 25% commission on delivery orders. Admin will have the ability to change the % or fee based on dispensary
perturbation.changed_text: Commission fees - 25% commission on delivery orders. Admin will not have the ability to change the % or fee based on dispensary
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing admin's abiltiy to alter commission fees, where originally there was an explicit allowance for administrators to modify the percentage or fee on a per-dispensary basis. Now there is a contradiction in whether the administrators can make the change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payouts, Subscription fees, flat fees, commissions fees § Commission fees - 25% commission on delivery orders. Admin will not have the ability to change the % or fee based on dispensary § Flat fees - $1 in store pickup orders through the app or website § Subscription Fee - $500 a month subscription per store · Top sponsors will pay $750 instead of $500 § Withdrawing Fee - $1 fee added to the existing daily pay fees § Delivery fee - 20% of delivery fee will go to Pelican. 80% will go to driver
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed administrators to change commission fees based on dispensary. The changed text explicitly removes this ability. The contradicted text, which includes the changed text, now states that the administrators cannot change the commission fees. This directly contradicts the original allowance, creating an inconsistency as to whether the administrators have the power to alter commission fees. This falls under the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, as different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.
